(ObjectUtils.isEmpty(postalCode) || postalCode.startsWith(CommonConstants.XX_VALUE))....explain
no.....its wrong make it something like ........(ObjectUtils.isNotEmpty(postalCode) &&  postalCode.startsWith(CommonConstants.XX_VALUE))
ObjectUtils.isNotEmpty....is not valid....fix it
(ObjectUtils.isEmpty(postalCode).....what will its return .......postalCode="XXsdfsdf"

If `postalCode` has the value "XXsdfsdf", calling `ObjectUtils.isEmpty(postalCode)` will return `false`. 

The `ObjectUtils.isEmpty()` method considers an object as empty if it is `null` or an empty String. Since "XXsdfsdf" is not an empty String, the method will return `false` to indicate that the `postalCode` is not empty in this case.

if ((ObjectUtils.nullSafeEquals(orderChannel.getAddress().getChildClientId(), “0”)) || (ObjectUtils.isEmpty(postalCode) || postalCode.startsWith(CommonConstants.XX_VALUE))) {.......(ObjectUtils.nullSafeEquals(orderChannel.getAddress().getChildClientId(), “0”)).....is this is condition not met .....then will code will enter this if condition of postalCode=“XXsdfsdf”
postalCode=“XXsdfsdf”....but this this is starting with XX

If `postalCode` is set to "XXsdfsdf" and the condition `postalCode.startsWith(CommonConstants.XX_VALUE)` is being evaluated, in this case, the condition will return `true`.

The `startsWith()` method checks whether the given string `postalCode` starts with the specific prefix defined in `CommonConstants.XX_VALUE`, which is "XX" in this case. Since "XXsdfsdf" does indeed start with "XX", the condition `postalCode.startsWith(CommonConstants.XX_VALUE)` will evaluate to `true`, regardless of the content following the "XX" prefix in the `postalCode` string.

Therefore, in this scenario, the condition `postalCode.startsWith(CommonConstants.XX_VALUE)` will return `true` when `postalCode` is set to "XXsdfsdf".

if ((ObjectUtils.nullSafeEquals(orderChannel.getAddress().getChildClientId(), “0”)) || (ObjectUtils.isEmpty(postalCode) || postalCode.startsWith("XX"))) {…(ObjectUtils.nullSafeEquals(orderChannel.getAddress().getChildClientId(), “0”))…is this is condition not met …then will code will enter this if condition of postalCode=“XXsdfsdf”
continue

If `(ObjectUtils.nullSafeEquals(orderChannel.getAddress().getChildClientId(), "0")` evaluates to `false`, and the second part of the condition `(ObjectUtils.isEmpty(postalCode) || postalCode.startsWith("XX"))` evaluates to `true` because `postalCode` is "XXsdfsdf" and it starts with "XX", then the code block inside the `if` statement will be executed.

Therefore, in this scenario:
- If `ObjectUtils.nullSafeEquals(orderChannel.getAddress().getChildClientId(), "0")` is `false`.
- And `postalCode` is set to "XXsdfsdf" which starts with "XX".

Then the code will enter the `if` condition and execute the code block enclosed within the `if` statement.
